strengths: 15+ years of fullstack experience with TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS (Optilyz, RIDE, re:cinq) — well beyond the 6+ year requirement
Hands-on AI agent development at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — a rare, explicitly required skill
Deep testing and craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and taught testing courses
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere on the resume, despite it being in the primary stack
Frontend history leans heavily on Ember.js; React evidence is limited to one role (Optilyz)
Java appears only briefly (robotic arms at Altom) and no Kotlin or Python — thin coverage of the secondary backend stack

key_factors.factor: Extensive TypeScript/Node fullstack track record with cloud and containers
Missing Nest.js and limited React/Kotlin depth

response.justification: Against the core competencies: fullstack TS/Node depth is strongly evidenced (pricing platform at Optilyz with TypeScript, AWS CDK, React, MongoDB); AI agent development is directly evidenced by MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure at re:cinq; cloud/containerization is evidenced by AWS CDK and Kubernetes-deployable GISCollective; testing expertise is evidenced by TDD practice and authored test frameworks, though Vitest/Jest are not named; databases are covered (MongoDB, MySQL). Gaps: Nest.js is absent, React exposure is comparatively recent, and Kotlin/Python are not listed. The candidate also holds a CS degree and shows strong communication through workshops and talks. The framework gaps are learnable given the demonstrated polyglot range (D, C++, Java, Swift, PHP), so the evidence supports a strong recommendation.
